article_title: China's Orbital Chenguang Lines Up $8.4 Billion to Build a Gigawatt Data Center in Sun-Synchronous Orbit

content_preview.title: China's Orbital Chenguang Lines Up $8.4 Billion to Build a Gigawatt Data Center in Sun-Synchronous Orbit
content_preview.summary: Beijing-backed Orbital Chenguang secured $8.4 billion in credit lines from twelve Chinese banks to build a gigawatt-scale orbital computing constellation by 2035, opening a state-aligned third front against SpaceX and Blue Origin in the race to put AI compute in orbit.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A Beijing-incubated startup has become the first Chinese company to formally enter the orbital data center race at scale, securing 57.7 billion yuan ($8.4 billion) in strategic credit lines from a dozen major Chinese banks. Beijing Orbital Twilight Technology Co., Ltd., known as Orbital Chenguang, announced the completion of its Pre-A1 equity round on April 20 alongside the credit framework, [according to SpaceNews](https://spacenews.com/china-backs-orbital-data-center-startup-with-...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong Space & Aerospace News piece. Both prior findings cleanly addressed.
editor_notes.source_verification: Re-verified Xinhua source-2: 'Zhejiang Lab launched the Three-Body Computing Constellation project' verbatim; '12 computing satellites of the Three-Body Computing Constellation successfully entered orbit' on May 14, 2025; Prometheus 'world's first space-based computing power cloud service platform for enterprises' verbatim. ADA Space's separate edge-computing 12-satellite launch is now attributed correctly. Lifecycle-emissions claim removed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Both prior findings resolved. Three-Body / Zhejiang Lab attribution corrected; ADA Space separated; carbon-credits aside removed.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE on re-review. Ready to merge.
